source: session log 2026-05-04 â€” duplicate PROFILE/PROJECTS/PREFERENCES sections visible in briefing
title: get_l3_context() emits duplicate section headers when category aliases collide
what_failed: Active context displays two PROFILE blocks, two PROJECTS blocks, two PREFERENCES blocks. Different L3 category names (e.g. 'profile' and 'permanent_profile', 'projects' and 'active_project') both render to the same display label. The code groups by raw key but outputs by label, so two separate keys that share a display label produce two identical headers.
where_failed: tools/tools_memory.py:794-815 â€” output loop uses raw category key for grouping but label_map for display, so two categories can collide on the same display label.
why_likely: Entries were stored with inconsistent category names over multiple sessions (some tools used 'profile', others 'permanent_profile'). The render path never normalised them to a single display group.
suggested_fix: In get_l3_context(), after building sections{}, merge all entries that share the same DISPLAY label into one list before rendering. Also add within-section content dedup: skip entries whose words are a >80% subset of another entry in the same group.
